title: Connection state and horizontal scaling
description: Persistent connections pin state to one instance, so broadcasting across a fleet needs a shared pub/sub backplane.
body: Use when scaling realtime beyond one process. A connection lives on one instance, so an event produced elsewhere will not reach it unless instances share a backplane (Redis/NATS pub/sub) that relays events to whichever instance holds each subscriber. Do not assume an in-memory subscriber map works across replicas, and beware that load balancers may break long connections without sticky/upgrade support. Actions: introduce a pub/sub backplane, keep per-connection state local but route events through it, and configure the LB for WebSocket upgrade or SSE. Validation signals include a cross-instance delivery test. Risk boundary: missing backplane means clients on other instances silently miss events.
